turned out to be renting a seat on the Chicago Board Options
Exchange and making markets for his own account. Like every
other trader
rule_text: Exploit price discrepancies between correlated instruments (futures vs. constituent stocks) by executing trades faster than the market can correct the mispricing.
entry_conditions: Futures price deviates from fair value of constituent stocks by >0.5%
Bid-ask spread is tight enough to allow profitable round trip
Both markets simultaneously accessible with <50ms latency
exit_conditions: Price convergence achieved or mispricing reverses

rule_text: Proactively identify and stress-test against low-probability but high-impact risks that others overlook, especially competitive threats in nascent opportunities.
entry_conditions: Identified structural or information advantage exists
Advantage is not yet widely exploited by industry
Competitive threat assessment: CRITICAL (assume 40%+ probability within 6 months)

source_citation: Flash Boys, Michael Lewis, Ch. CONTENTS/Hidden in Plain Sight, pp. 15-296
raw_chunk_text:
HIDDEN IN PLAIN SIGHT
headlines in the newspapers: Barksdale Makes Billions Sell-
ing Out Ordinary American Investor. They hired an industry
consultant named Larry Tabb, who had caught Jim Barksdale’s
rule_text: A trader should exploit microscopic price discrepancies between the same instrument across geographic markets (cash-futures or regional arbitrage) when the aggregate profit potential from cumulative small trades justifies infrastructure investment.
entry_conditions: Price discrepancy between primary and secondary market > (median_spread + 2σ)
Historical frequency of such discrepancies ≥ 100 per trading day
Infrastructure cost amortization period ≤ 5 years at current volume
exit_conditions: Discrepancy converges to within 0.5σ of median
